title: Fix CompiledModule content hash to include actual content
analysis: The bug is in two locations: (1) compile() at lib.rs:612 computes CompiledModule content_hash from only the module path string, and (2) compile_structure() at lib.rs:803 computes TopologyTemplate content_hash from only the entity name. The same bug is replicated in the test-support builders (TopologyTemplateBuilder::build at builders.rs:259 and CompiledModuleBuilder::build at builders.rs:327). The fix uses ContentHash::combine/combine_all to fold in actual content: for TopologyTemplate, combine the name hash with hashes from all value cell default expressions, constraint expressions, and sub_component content_hashes; for CompiledModule, combine the path hash with all template content_hashes and import path hashes. The existing content_hash fields on CompiledExpr and SubComponentDecl already carry correct per-item hashes, so we just need to aggregate them. Tests use reify_syntax::parse() to compile real source text, then compare hashes across different inputs. No existing tests hardcode specific hash values from compile() — the boundary1_consumer tests construct ParsedModule manually with explicit hashes that are passed through, and boundary2_producer's content_hashes_present test just checks non-zero, so it will still pass.

steps.description: Write failing test: two different source texts with same module path produce different CompiledModule content_hashes. In boundary2_producer.rs, create test 'different_content_same_path_different_hash' that parses two sources (e.g. bracket with width=80mm vs width=120mm) with the same ModulePath, compiles both, and asserts their content_hashes differ. This will FAIL because current hash only uses path.

Write failing test: same source compiled twice produces identical content_hashes (determinism). In boundary2_producer.rs, create test 'same_content_same_hash_deterministic' that parses and compiles the same source twice and asserts content_hashes are equal. This should PASS even before the fix (path-only hash is deterministic), serving as a regression guard.

Write failing test: changing a param default value changes content_hash. Create test 'param_default_change_changes_hash' — compile bracket source with thickness=5mm vs a version with thickness=10mm (same path), assert module content_hashes differ. Will FAIL with current path-only hash.

Write failing test: adding/removing a constraint changes content_hash. Create test 'add_constraint_changes_hash' — compile one source with 2 constraints and another with 3 constraints (same path and same params), assert module content_hashes differ. Will FAIL with current path-only hash.

Fix compile_structure() in lib.rs: replace `ContentHash::of_str(entity_name)` with a proper content hash that combines the name hash with hashes from all compiled content. Use ContentHash::combine_all over: (1) name hash, (2) each value_cell's default_expr.content_hash (or a sentinel ContentHash(0) for None), (3) each constraint's expr.content_hash, (4) each sub_component's content_hash. This makes TopologyTemplate hashes content-sensitive.

Fix compile() in lib.rs: replace `ContentHash::of_str(&format!("{}", parsed.path))` with a combined hash of: (1) the path hash, (2) all template content_hashes (via combine_all), (3) import path hashes. This makes CompiledModule hashes content-sensitive. All 4 new tests should now pass.

Fix TopologyTemplateBuilder::build() in test-support/src/builders.rs: replace `ContentHash::of_str(&self.name)` with a combined hash matching the same pattern as compile_structure() — fold in value_cells default_expr hashes, constraint expr hashes, and sub_components content hashes.

Fix CompiledModuleBuilder::build() in test-support/src/builders.rs: replace `ContentHash::of_str(&format!("{}", self.path))` with a combined hash matching compile() — fold in template content_hashes and import path hashes.
Run full test suite for reify-compiler and reify-test-support crates to verify all existing tests still pass and all 4 new tests pass. Fix any regressions if found.

design_decisions.decision: Combine hashes using ContentHash::combine_all over an iterator of sub-hashes, not by serializing the entire struct to a string
design_decisions.rationale: Using combine_all is the established pattern in the codebase (see compile_expr which builds content_hash via combine). It's efficient (no allocation for serialization) and composable. The alternative of hashing a Debug/Display string would be fragile and dependent on formatting.

design_decisions.decision: Include a sentinel hash (ContentHash(0)) for value cells with no default_expr (None) rather than skipping them
design_decisions.rationale: If we skip None entries, adding an auto param (which has no default_expr) to an existing structure wouldn't change the hash if the other params remained the same. Using a sentinel ensures the hash reflects the full structure of the template including the count and position of value cells.

design_decisions.decision: Include import paths in the CompiledModule hash
design_decisions.rationale: Adding/removing an import changes the module's semantics even if templates are unchanged. This is important for future module resolution — when imports actually resolve, their content will flow into template compilation. Including import paths now prevents cache staleness when import resolution is implemented.

design_decisions.decision: Do NOT include diagnostics in the content hash
design_decisions.rationale: Diagnostics are derived from the content — they're warnings/errors about the content, not part of it. Including them would make the hash unstable if diagnostic messages change (e.g., improved error text). The actual compiled content (templates, imports) is what matters for cache invalidation.

design_decisions.decision: Fix the test-support builders to match the compiler's hashing logic
design_decisions.rationale: The builders are used to construct expected CompiledModules in tests. If the builder produces path-only hashes while the compiler produces content-sensitive hashes, comparisons in tests would fail or, worse, silently use wrong hashes. Consistency between compiler and test infrastructure is essential.

design_decisions.decision: Use parse-then-compile (not manually constructed ParsedModule) for the new hash tests
design_decisions.rationale: Using reify_syntax::parse() with real source text (like the existing compile_auto_param test does) is more realistic and less brittle than manually constructing AST nodes. It also tests the full pipeline and ensures the parser's content_hash fields feed correctly into the compiler's hash.

reuse.how: Use combine_all to fold template hashes, value cell hashes, constraint hashes, and import hashes into a single module/template content hash
Each compiled expression already carries a content_hash. Use value_cell.default_expr.content_hash and constraint.expr.content_hash as inputs to the template hash
Sub-components already have content_hash from the parser. Include these in the TopologyTemplate hash via combine_all
Use this to generate two different source texts with the same module path for the 'different content, different hash' test
Parse real source text in tests (same pattern as compile_auto_param and e2e_stdlib_function_in_let_binding tests) rather than manually constructing ParsedModule
Follow the established test style — use reify_test_support fixtures, reify_syntax::parse(), and assert on CompiledModule properties
